purpose: Eighth AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering a completed neighbor-target command buffer that remains at the append boundary.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular unsupported command-buffer node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular unsupported command-buffer node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular unsupported command-buffer node.

layers.summary: A matching one-hot standard input completes buffer 01001, decodes it as neighbor-a/stem-init, and preserves it at the append boundary.
layers.boundary: This layer states only the unsupported completed command-buffer boundary; it does not route neighbor commands or execute self-target non-init commands.

layers.summary: The unsupported command-buffer node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one unsupported completed command-buffer append.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0041 append-boundary claim and adds no neighbor delivery.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested append boundary.

routed_signal_flow: completed command buffer preserved at append boundary
